content:
- Ramki Gaddipati's Office Hours: Digital Twin Meeting Analysis

- II. Overall Context of the Conversation/Call

- This meeting was a regular India Customer Success check-in focused on reviewing account and customer program health, issue management, and support process improvements across several key Zeta programs, most notably Pixel, Sigma, and Flexi. The group discussed volumes of customer support issues, current reporting and operations monitoring processes, and blockers in delivering product/engineering solutions to production.

- Ramki was actively involved in guiding escalation processes, championing root cause reduction for technical ticket volumes, and aligning support and engineering expectations for faster resolution. Discussions included the need for actionable and automated health reporting, strengthening product operations capabilities, audit readiness (with internal and customer-facing demo expectations), and improving communications/tracking with customer teams and the bank. There was emphasis on cross-program consistency—extracting best practices (such as publishing resolved issues) and focusing operational/process improvements equally across all major lines of business.

- IV. Key Takeaways from the Call

- Automated Health Reporting Mandated: Strong push for automating daily and summary health checklists, to be surfaced in OP Center. Critical manual spreadsheets/processes must be replaced by systemized automation. (“Should have an automated snapshot…call it Master Reconciliation Report.”)

- Top Issue Generator Fixes as Sprint Commitment: Consensus that each product/SaaS team must commit to resolving at least top-two high-frequency causes of tickets/issues per sprint—applies to engineering, operations, and all relevant stakeholders. (“This is a promise Pixel has made…similar support should be there from every SaaS.”)

- Support Team Right-sizing & Capacity Analysis: Ramki insisted on benchmarking the Pixel customer support staffing model against similar programs (particularly Plat CL2), with action expected to assess comparative headcount hygiene. (“See how it compares with Plat CL2 support…”)

- Product Operations Enablement (Op Center for Product Ops): There was a directive to transition product operations from ad hoc/manual script execution (often on individuals’ machines or with privileged tokens) to a mature, access-controlled, and auditable self-service model—rolling out Jupyter Notebook-based runbooks. (“We need an Op Center for our product operations, not just customer OP Center.”)

- Audit Center Demo and Readiness Critical: The group agreed urgent internal demonstrations of the Audit Center are required before involving the bank; thorough stakeholder involvement and documentation to close audit/compliance gaps were highlighted by Ramki and others.

- Cross-Team Demo Participation Enforcement: Ramki stressed that all relevant stakeholders (named individually) must attend key demos, especially for audit-critical features and process showcases; there was no room for exceptions. (“They should mandatory go through it...Gopi is not an exception…”)

- V. Ramki's Insights

1. Support Staffing & Process Benchmarking:
- Ramki emphasized the importance of sizing and modeling the Pixel customer support team against comparative internal groups (e.g., Plat CL2), stressing the need for data-driven rightsizing and adequate coverage. His approach was solution-oriented but acknowledged that simple bandwidth increases may not always solve root issues—process changes, role restructuring, and engineering alignment are equally needed.

2. Defect Reduction as a Program Standard:
- Ramki articulated a clear expectation that all SaaS and engineering units must establish a routine where addressing top issue generators is part of every sprint cycle. He was assertive that this systematic approach isn’t negotiable and reinforced it with the requirement to publish resolved issue lists. He was careful to state that this is about both bug numbers and broader L2 support drivers.

3. Automated, Auditable Reporting:
- Ramki was insistent on the migration from manual/excel-based health and defect reporting to automated, centralized solutions (OP Center), showing a vision for scalable, reliable data visibility. He ideated the “Master Reconciliation Report” as the centerpiece, showing his preference for structural, audit-proof processes.

4. Process and Role Ownership Clarity in Operations:
- Ramki directed that operational interventions (e.g., for account reinstatement, EMI corrections) should be led by empowered product operations teams and only escalate to SRE or engineering when absolutely necessary, highlighting the need for documented, access-controlled runbooks, and a move away from informal, potentially risky practices.

5. Audit and Demo Rigor:
- He was highly specific about making all support and product ops teams accountable for readiness and stakeholder participation; he called out by name the necessity for broad attendance/engagement in audit center and OP Center demos and a zero-tolerance stance against skipping these critical engagement checkpoints.

6. On Issue Classification and Stakeholder Collaboration:
- He distinguished between “issues” and “change/service requests,” cautioning against labeling avoidable or expected business requests as defects. Ramki promoted mature, business-driven reporting/communication and insisted on clarity/up-to-date status in stakeholder-facing materials.

- VI. Ramki's Suggested Action Items

1. Automate Daily and Summary Health Reports in OP Center
- Task: Develop/automate daily summary reports ("Master Reconciliation Report") consolidating all customer health checks, defect states, and tracked activities into OP Center (eliminating reliance on manual Excel sheets).
- Assigned To: Gayatri (priority), with Uttam’s input; Kumaran to drive coordination.
- Ramki's Instructions on Layout/Methodology: The focus should be on full automation, direct integration with operational data, and alignment with existing bank checklists. Duplicate effort is discouraged; all required reporting must converge here.
- Timeline Mentioned by Ramki: Immediate/priority (no specific date but flagged as high-urgency).

2. Benchmark and Adjust Customer Support Headcount/Processes
- Task: Compare current Pixel support/L2 structure and headcount against Plat CL2; adjust for adequacy.
- Assigned To: Kumaran (with Navakanth’s support as data source).
- Ramki's Instructions on Layout/Methodology: Perform an apples-to-apples, data-driven comparison; identify and address coverage or process gaps, not just by adding headcount but by optimizing flows.
- Timeline Mentioned by Ramki: No explicit date; implied as soon as feasible.

3. Institutionalize "Top Two Issues" Closure per Sprint
- Task: Each SaaS/product line (engineering and operations) to commit and deliver closure of at least top two most impactful support drivers/issues per sprint.
- Assigned To: All SaaS/product line leads (Pixel, ITP, Credit, etc.); Kumaran, Gireesh, Arvind—ownership for ensuring publication.
- Ramki's Instructions on Layout/Methodology: Track, publish, and socialize resolved items for visibility (both bugs and broader support drivers); “publish what all we have resolved next time” for ongoing recognition.
- Timeline Mentioned by Ramki: Ongoing; every sprint.

4. Mature Product Operations Enablement & Workflow Automation
- Task: Transition product operations activities (e.g., account reinstatement, direct customer fixes) from manual/scripted approaches to formal tools, specifically using Jupyter Notebook-based runbooks accessible via Op Center.
- Assigned To: Product operations leadership (Kumaran, Ashish, Gireesh); Ramesh and others as implementation support.
- Ramki's Instructions on Layout/Methodology: Expand automation coverage across all tenants, migrate from local/script/token-based interventions, enforce access controls, and minimize risk while ensuring flexibility (Node.js/Python support).
- Timeline Mentioned by Ramki: Initiation immediate, phased rollout (no spec date, but “already in production in some areas”).

5. Enforce Full Stakeholder Attendance in Key Demos (Audit/OP Center)
- Task: Mandate participation of all relevant stakeholders (names called out) for critical feature walkthroughs and demos for audit readiness; ensure no exceptions.
- Assigned To: Kumaran to coordinate, Gireesh/Arvind to monitor attendance.
- Ramki's Instructions on Layout/Methodology: Pre-announce, track attendance; escalate non-participation. Bank/BTC/engineering/ops, named individuals (e.g., Prasad, Shanky, Suresh, Rekha, Travan, Gopi, Uttam, Alok) are to be included.
- Timeline Mentioned by Ramki: Prior to next customer/bank demo; urgency implied.

6. Clarify Issue Classification in Demos and Reports
- Task: Update demo decks and reporting to properly distinguish between defects/issues and change/service requests; ensure clarity for all external stakeholders.
- Assigned To: Kumaran, Navakanth, Sandesh.
- Ramki's Instructions on Layout/Methodology: Avoid mislabeling; ensure supporting details for “red” status are directly visible in all reports/shared decks.
- Timeline Mentioned by Ramki: Ongoing, to apply before each major review/demo.

- VII. Jira Ticket

1. Scope: Automate Daily (Master Reconciliation) Health Reporting in OP Center
- Assignee: Gayatri (with Kumaran/Uttam’s coordination)
- Justification: Directly supports Ramki's top action for automation and audit compliance, replaces risky/inefficient manual health tracking with systematic, enterprise-level reporting.

2. Scope: Benchmark and Adjust Pixel Customer Support Staffing Model
- Assignee: Kumaran (Navakanth for support)
- Justification: Enables process and resource optimization for customer/l2 support, as directly requested by Ramki; aligns with capacity and workload management best practices.

- Other action items (issue closure per sprint, stakeholder attendance enforcement, demo/report content changes) are not suited for Jira due to their recurring/process-driven, rather than project-specific, nature, and/or being broad organizational mandates rather than individual deliverables.

- VIII. Follow Up Questions/Suggestions

- Review progress on automating the Master Reconciliation Report in the next call, with a targeted walkthrough of OP Center integration.
- Ensure stakeholder participation lists for critical demos are circulated in advance, with a system for tracking and escalating missing participants.
- Confirm new process for surfacing and publishing sprint-closed top issues is being implemented uniformly across all SaaS/product lines.
- Request update from Gayatri/Uttam on bank checklist alignment and gap closure for all daily production reporting.
- Seek feedback from product operations team on efficacy and adoption of Jupyter Notebook-based runbooks versus prior manual methods.

entities: Customer Success, Program Health, Account status tracking, Ticket closure rates, Technical declines, Operational monitoring, Daily health check, Master Reconciliation Report, Audit center, Product operations enablement, Card renewal/expiry, Access controls, Internal/external demo, Cross-team coordination, Automation, Defect triage, Stakeholder engagement, Reporting improvements, Service request workflows

title: 2025-03-17_India Customer Success Check-in - Fortnighly (4)
